dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK Prime edge security — TLS/SSL posture weak
dispatches.current_truth: Prime is now attached to guests.hostel-positano.com, but the live Cloudflare zone still uses ssl=flexible and min_tls_version=1.0. Cloudflare Access is not enabled, and there is no visible managed-firewall entrypoint in the zone audit. That is not an acceptable edge posture for a public-facing guest app handling booking-linked access flows.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find the production hardening changes for the Prime edge surface: move the zone to Full (strict), raise minimum TLS to 1.2 or higher, confirm certificate/origin behavior for Pages, and define the right WAF/Access posture for public guest routes versus staff-only surfaces.
adjacent_later: Review managed WAF and bot controls after TLS posture is corrected
Decide whether staff/admin paths should move behind Cloudflare Access
evidence_refs: operator-stated: Prime is public facing and must be extremely secure
live-audit: Cloudflare zone hostel-positano.com checked 2026-03-06 shows ssl=flexible
live-audit: Cloudflare zone hostel-positano.com checked 2026-03-06 shows min_tls_version=1.0
live-audit: Cloudflare Access API reports access.api.error.not_enabled for hostel-positano.com
infra-ref: guests.hostel-positano.com is the newly activated Prime custom domain
dispatches.why: The Prime guest portal has just been promoted onto a public custom domain, so the Cloudflare edge posture is now part of the real production attack surface rather than an internal preview detail. Leaving the zone on flexible SSL and TLS 1.0 materially weakens transport security and creates ambiguity about what other edge protections are actually in force.

intended_outcome.statement: A planning-ready hardening path exists for the Prime public edge surface covering Full (strict) TLS, modern minimum TLS version, certificate/origin implications for Pages, and the correct WAF or Access boundaries for public guest routes versus staff-only routes.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK Prime guest access — token/session and door-code boundary weak
dispatches.current_truth: Prime guest access is currently based on a URL token that is verified with surname and then stored in localStorage. The portal treats network_error as good enough to continue. The check-in-code endpoint still exposes and generates door codes by raw uuid and checkout date without requiring a validated guest session. Those choices make the public guest surface materially weaker than the repo's staff-side security posture.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find the correct hardening design for guest access: replace browser-stored guest auth with a fail-closed server session, require session-bound authorization for door-code flows, tighten booking lookup and token issuance, and define the browser/header controls needed for a public guest portal.
adjacent_later: Review direct-message and assistant endpoints after the guest session contract is hardened
Revisit CORS and response-header policy once the session model is defined
evidence_refs: operator-stated: Prime is public facing and must be extremely secure
code-ref: apps/prime/src/app/g/page.tsx stores prime_guest_token and related identity fields in localStorage after surname verification
code-ref: apps/prime/src/app/portal/page.tsx treats validateGuestToken() network_error as allowed for portal continuation
code-ref: apps/prime/functions/api/check-in-code.ts exposes GET and POST door-code flows by uuid without validateGuestSessionToken()
code-ref: apps/prime/functions/api/find-booking.ts issues guest deep links from bookingRef plus surname with only per-IP KV throttling
dispatches.why: The app has moved onto a public custom domain, and the current guest-access design still relies on a convenience-oriented token handoff rather than a strongly bounded session model. That was tolerable while the app was effectively preview-grade, but it is now the wrong security posture for guest-linked booking data, door access instructions, and any future expansion of the portal surface.

intended_outcome.statement: A planning-ready hardening path exists for Prime guest access covering server-bound guest sessions, fail-closed portal validation, authenticated door-code retrieval, stronger booking-link issuance controls, and the browser security controls needed for a public guest portal.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK reception inbox — app-native draft-first email desk
dispatches.current_truth: Brikette email handling is still centered on Gmail labels, local MCP tooling, and narrow reception draft routes. There is no app-native inbox in reception, the hosted app has no canonical thread state, and the current Gmail auth/audit model assumes local filesystem tokens and logs rather than a hosted staff workflow.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find the minimum hosted architecture for a reception-native inbox: D1-backed canonical thread state, admission rules that keep spam and no-response mail out of the working inbox, draft-review-send lifecycle through Gmail backend delivery, and a low-volume polling/manual-refresh sync model that avoids Google admin dependencies.
adjacent_later: Add richer multi-user ownership and collision handling after single-user-first workflow is stable
Evaluate push sync and attachment handling only after v1 proves operational value
evidence_refs: operator-stated: email volume usually stays under 100 and drops sharply during five closed months
operator-stated: attachments are out of scope, replies must stay draft-first, and no Google admin access is available
code-ref: packages/mcp-server/src/clients/gmail.ts - current Gmail client relies on credentials.json and token.json on local filesystem
code-ref: packages/mcp-server/src/tools/gmail-shared.ts - audit and lock persistence are file-backed and local-process oriented
code-ref: apps/reception/wrangler.toml - reception is deployed as a Cloudflare Worker
dispatches.why: The current Brikette email workflow is workable for ad hoc operator use, but it is not a clean staff-facing inbox: queue state lives in Gmail labels, the hosted reception app only exposes narrow draft routes, and the Gmail backend assumptions are still local-process oriented. Because volume is low and the workflow is explicitly draft-first, there is now a realistic path to replace Gmail as the day-to-day UI without buying a third-party help desk, provided the hosted design avoids the current local-filesystem assumptions.

intended_outcome.statement: A planning-ready architecture exists for a reception-native inbox that admits only actionable emails, stores thread state in app-native records, supports draft-review-send flow through Gmail backend delivery, and fits the current Cloudflare-hosted setup without requiring attachments or Google admin features.

dispatches.area_anchor: PLAT startup loop — markdown used as machine input, downstream code reverse-parses structure
dispatches.current_truth: generate-process-improvements.ts re-parses results-review.user.md to reconstruct title/body pairs and run deterministic classification. The model pays tokens authoring prose that downstream code immediately reverse-parses back into structure. This creates parser fragility and unnecessarily inflates prompt context.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find the right sidecar schema for results-review.signals.json and pattern-reflection.entries.json. Design how generate-process-improvements.ts and other consumers would prefer the structured sidecar over markdown parsing, while keeping current human-readable .user.md artifacts unchanged for operator review.
adjacent_later: Extend lp-do-build to emit .signals.json and .entries.json sidecars alongside existing .user.md artifacts
Audit other downstream consumers of results-review.user.md for similar reverse-parse patterns
evidence_refs: operator-stated: model authors prose that downstream code immediately parses back into structure — invert to emit machine data first, render markdown for humans from that
code-ref: scripts/src/startup-loop/generate-process-improvements.ts:426 — markdown re-parse to reconstruct title/body pairs
code-ref: scripts/src/startup-loop/generate-process-improvements.ts:568 — deterministic classification run on parsed markdown
dispatches.why: Using markdown as the machine-readable interface between the loop's build phase and its reporting layer means the model pays to author prose that code then strips and re-parses. A canonical JSON sidecar emitted alongside the human-readable artifact removes this round-trip, reduces prompt context size, and eliminates parser fragility without changing the operator-facing output.

intended_outcome.statement: results-review.signals.json and pattern-reflection.entries.json sidecars are defined with a canonical schema; generate-process-improvements.ts reads from the sidecar when present and falls back to markdown parsing; existing .user.md artifacts remain unchanged for operator review.

dispatches.area_anchor: PLAT results-review prefill — model pays for signal extraction that code could do deterministically
dispatches.current_truth: The results-review prefill hardcodes all five idea categories to None and leaves Observed Outcomes as a stub, then asks the model (via codemoot) to scan build context and fill them. Most of that first-pass evidence collection is deterministic: changed package detection, new SKILL.md detection, startup-loop contract/spec changes, standing-registry intersections, schema/lint/validator additions, and build/task completion bullets — none of which requires model judgment.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find which signal types can be lifted into deterministic pre-extraction code in lp-do-build-results-review-prefill.ts, and how to structure the residue-only model step that handles genuinely ambiguous synthesis after deterministic signals are populated.
adjacent_later: Wire deterministic signal output into results-review.signals.json sidecar (dependent on packet 0993)
Reduce codemoot prompt size once deterministic signals are pre-populated
evidence_refs: operator-stated: results-review spends model tokens on deterministic extraction rather than judgment — changed package detection, SKILL.md detection, contract changes, registry intersections are all code-extractable
code-ref: scripts/src/startup-loop/lp-do-build-results-review-prefill.ts:97 — all five idea categories hardcoded to None
code-ref: scripts/src/startup-loop/lp-do-build-results-review-prefill.ts:187 — Observed Outcomes left as stub for model to populate
code-ref: .claude/skills/lp-do-build/SKILL.md:206 — preferred path invokes codemoot to scan and fill categories
dispatches.why: The model is being used as a first-pass file scanner for signals that a short TypeScript routine could extract faster, more reliably, and at zero token cost. Lifting deterministic signals into code keeps the model step focused on ambiguous synthesis — the one task that actually requires judgment.

intended_outcome.statement: A deterministic pre-extraction step in lp-do-build-results-review-prefill.ts populates changed-package, new-SKILL.md, contract/spec change, registry-intersection, schema/validator, and task-completion signals before any model call; the model step receives only unresolved ambiguous items for synthesis.

dispatches.area_anchor: PLAT pattern-reflection — category/evidence lost at handoff, noop refinement not skipped
dispatches.current_truth: extractCurrentIdeas() returns each idea with category: undefined, so the pattern-reflection prefill falls back to unclassified for every idea and the build skill asks the model to correct category classifications. Additionally, the refinement step runs unconditionally even when the prefill has already produced a complete valid empty artifact (zero ideas), paying model tokens for a noop pass.

adjacent_later: Once results-review.signals.json sidecar exists, source category from structured data rather than re-parsing markdown
evidence_refs: operator-stated: direct token leak — model reclassifies ideas that already have categories and runs refinement on complete empty artifacts
code-ref: scripts/src/startup-loop/lp-do-build-pattern-reflection-prefill.ts:210 — prefill falls back to unclassified
code-ref: .claude/skills/lp-do-build/SKILL.md:235 — build skill asks model to correct category classifications
code-ref: .claude/skills/lp-do-build/SKILL.md:239 — no skip gate before refinement step

dispatches.area_anchor: PLAT Claude Bash permissions - surface too broad for lock use
dispatches.current_truth: Claude currently has a broad Bash permission surface and safety is applied afterward through hooks, deny lists, and wrappers. That makes shell a default transport for discovery and file operations even though the actual need is narrow writer-lock and command execution support.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find the minimum Bash permission surface: identify which guarded write and validation commands truly require shell execution, which discovery paths should move to Read, Glob, Grep, or MCP, and what permission or hook changes would safely reverse the recent broad allowance.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: recent Bash allowance for file locking and guarding appears to have increased Claude errors and should likely be reversed
agent-verified: .claude/settings.json broadly hooks and permits Bash while safety is layered afterward
agent-verified: lock wrappers exist in scripts/agents/integrator-shell.sh and scripts/agents/with-writer-lock.sh, so the write use case is narrower than general shell access
dispatches.why: Broad shell access increases failure modes and policy drift. Claude should only use shell when a real process must run or a guarded writer path is required.

intended_outcome.statement: Claude Bash access is narrowed to an executor-only surface that preserves guarded write and validation flows without making shell the default tool.

dispatches.area_anchor: PLAT Claude tool selection - no explicit read-first protocol
dispatches.current_truth: Repo guidance says to read before editing, but there is no short canonical tool ladder that tells Claude when to use Read, Glob, Grep, MCP language tools, or Bash. That leaves tool choice implicit and inconsistent.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find the correct Claude tool order and decision boundaries: Read for file contents, Glob for discovery, Grep for search, MCP tools for semantic TypeScript work, and Bash only for execution or guarded repo commands. Then map the rule into the right docs and permission surfaces.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: we should ensure the correct tool calls are made and define what those tool calls should be, for example Read, Glob, Grep, and Bash
agent-verified: docs/INDEX_FOR_CLAUDE.md and .claude/SETUP_COMPLETE.md already say to read before editing
agent-verified: docs/ide/agent-language-intelligence-guide.md already defines MCP-first behavior for TypeScript diagnostics
dispatches.why: The tool policy is currently underspecified, so Claude falls back to Bash too often. A clear tool ladder should reduce errors and make sessions more deterministic.

intended_outcome.statement: Claude-facing guidance defines a canonical tool-selection ladder and the related permission model so discovery and semantic work happen without unnecessary shell calls.

dispatches.area_anchor: PLAT agent failure contract - precise next-step instructions
dispatches.current_truth: Many blocked or failed agent paths surface the prohibition or error but do not always state exactly what to do next. That leaves room for reattempt loops, alternative commands that will fail for the same reason, and wasted tool fan-out.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find a repo-wide failure-instruction contract for agent-facing errors and guards: define the minimum required fields for every blocked message, identify the highest-leverage guard surfaces to update first, and map exact next-step guidance that prevents retry loops and adjacent impossible actions.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: fail messages must include very precise instructions so agents do not get stuck in doom-loop retries or fan out to other non-working approaches
agent-verified: AGENTS.md already enforces explicit IF BLOCKED sections for procedural guidance, proving the repo values prescriptive fallback instructions
agent-verified: repo plans already use actionable failure contract language for sync/status and retry-loop prevention, but the pattern is not yet a universal agent-failure standard
dispatches.why: A blocked or failed path should terminate ambiguity, not create more of it. Precise next-step instructions are an efficiency control that prevents retry loops and wasted tool exploration.

intended_outcome.statement: Agent-facing failure messages across guards and blocked operations follow a precise contract that says whether retry is valid, what exact next step to take, and when to stop escalating locally.

dispatches.area_anchor: xa-uploader product form — false autosave state, silent step transition, delete placement
dispatches.current_truth: CatalogProductForm initialises autosaveStatus to 'saved' on fresh/new product load before any actual save has occurred, misleading the operator. intended_outcome.statement: Deliver a plan and implementation that (1) initialises autosave status to 'unsaved' or a neutral state on fresh/new product load, (2) adds visible feedback (toast or inline message) when the save-and-advance transition fires, and (3) relocates the delete button to the action footer with a confirmation step.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK gmail pipeline — inquiry draft dedup missing at tool level
evidence_refs: operator-stated: guard review found gmail_create_draft has no duplicate check — session crash after draft creation but before gmail_mark_processed applies terminal label leaves email re-exposed to queue, causing duplicate drafts on next run
packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/gmail-booking-dedup.test.ts (dedup exists only for booking reservation path, not inquiry path)
dispatches.why: The booking reservation path has checkBookingRefDuplicate() but the customer inquiry path (gmail_create_draft called from ops-inbox) has no equivalent guard. A session crash between draft creation and gmail_mark_processed leaves the email with no terminal label, so the next gmail_organize_inbox run re-queues it. The operator then processes it again, producing a second draft in Gmail for the same customer email.

intended_outcome.statement: gmail_create_draft checks for an existing draft on the same email thread before creating a new one and skips or warns if one already exists, preventing duplicate inquiry response drafts from session crash recovery.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK gmail pipeline — In-Progress recovery not auto-scheduled
evidence_refs: operator-stated: guard review found gmail_reconcile_in_progress exists but is never called automatically — emails stuck In-Progress after session crash are invisible to the queue until operator manually runs the tool
packages/mcp-server/src/tools/gmail-reconciliation.ts (handleReconcileInProgress — full implementation, not wired to any schedule)
packages/mcp-server/src/tools/gmail.ts (TERMINAL_LABELS excludes PROCESSING label from re-scan — correct, but means stuck emails disappear silently)
dispatches.why: When an ops-inbox session crashes mid-processing, the email retains the In-Progress label (Brikette/Queue/In-Progress). Because In-Progress is in TERMINAL_LABELS, gmail_organize_inbox will never re-queue it. The email silently disappears from the active queue until gmail_reconcile_in_progress is run manually. Operators are unlikely to remember to run this tool, so stuck emails accumulate over time and customer messages go unanswered.

intended_outcome.statement: Stale In-Progress emails are automatically recovered at the start of each ops-inbox session (or via a scheduled hook), so no customer email can become permanently invisible to the queue due to a session crash.

dispatches.why: ensureLabelMap creates Gmail labels on demand but never removes stale or renamed ones. intended_outcome.statement: A label audit/cleanup tool or startup check identifies orphaned Brikette labels (those not in the current REQUIRED_LABELS set) and either removes them or flags them for operator review, keeping the Gmail label hierarchy clean.

dispatches.area_anchor: lp-do workflow rehearsal boundary — pre-build rehearsal, post-build reflection only
dispatches.current_truth: The lp-do workflow already has upstream structural simulation steps in fact-find, plan, and critique, and lp-do-build already owns execution plus post-build reflection artifacts. adjacent_later: Historical audit of archived plans to measure what net-new issues delivery rehearsal would have caught beyond critique.

dispatches.current_truth: Smoke test confirmed /it/prenota redirects to /en — IT-speaking visitors land on an English booking page. slug-map.ts maps book → prenota for IT locale. _redirects contains a 200 rewrite rule /it/prenota → /it/book (generated by generate-static-export-redirects.ts). book is listed in STATIC_EXPORT_SECTION_KEYS and generateStaticParams() in book/page.tsx calls generateLangParams() which includes all supportedLngs including it. Root cause is unconfirmed: either (1) the static export does not produce /it/book/index.html (Cloudflare Pages 200 rewrite target missing → falls through to catch-all → /en), or (2) the production Worker middleware does not translate prenota → book before routing.

dispatches.current_truth: The test-execution-resource-governor fact-find and plan exist (status: Ready-for-planning) and are scoped to local admission control — throttling, concurrency caps, and command-path guarding. dispatches.area_anchor: lp-do-plan/fact-find/critique skills — simulation walk-through replaces local test runs for TDD validation
dispatches.current_truth: lp-do-plan produces implementation plans but has no built-in step where the agent simulates or traces through the proposed changes step-by-step before emitting tasks. lp-do-fact-find gathers evidence and scopes work but does not simulate how a proposed system would behave end-to-end. lp-do-critique critiques documents but does not walk through a simulated execution path of the thing being critiqued. When TDD-style validation is needed during planning, the only mechanism is to actually run tests locally — which is expensive and adds to local resource pressure. There is no agent-driven simulation protocol in any of the three skills today. The resource offload idea (IDEA-DISPATCH-20260227-0034) removes local tests entirely, which makes a simulation-based substitute for TDD validation during planning strictly necessary.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find to design a simulation-based TDD validation protocol for lp-do-plan, lp-do-fact-find, and lp-do-critique: (1) define 'simulation' precisely in this context — is it a structured agent walk-through of the proposed task sequence, checking each task for missing preconditions, type errors, API contract mismatches, missing config, or integration failures? How formal should the trace be?; (2) determine insertion points in each skill — for lp-do-plan, does simulation run after the plan is drafted but before it is emitted to the operator? For lp-do-fact-find, does the agent simulate the investigation scope to check for gaps in coverage? For lp-do-critique, does the agent simulate executing what is being critiqued to surface failure modes?; (3) define what constitutes a simulation 'issue' — what categories of problem can an agent catch without running code (missing data dependency, API signature mismatch, circular task ordering, undefined config key, etc.) and what are the limits of simulation vs actual test execution?; (4) decide whether the simulation output is a hard gate (blocks plan emission) or an advisory block in the output; (5) read the current SKILL.md files for all three skills and identify exact protocol sections where simulation steps would be inserted; (6) produce a planning brief with specific SKILL.md edits for each of the three skills plus any shared simulation-protocol doc.

Simulation output format standardisation so critique and plan results can be compared across runs

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK Prime digital assistant — keyword fallback, needs OpenAI LLM upgrade
dispatches.current_truth: Prime digital assistant is fully client-side keyword matching. Six categories (~18 keyword triggers) cover booking, extensions, experiences, food, bag drop, and transport. Any query that does not match a keyword returns a static fallback: 'I do not have a direct answer for that yet. Use Booking Details or ask reception.' No API call is made, no LLM is involved, no AI capability exists. The page has no loading state. No preset query buttons. Single-exchange only — each submit replaces the previous answer. No booking context is passed into the answer logic. The legacy app called POST https://hostel-positano.com/primetime/api/processQuery (now decommissioned) which was an OpenAI-backed backend — no system prompt or grounding config is recoverable.

(3) Safety fallback when OpenAI signals out-of-scope or call fails — serve a clear escalation message, never the old dead-end generic fallback. (4) Rate-limit via existing kv-rate-limit.ts pattern. (5) Restore multi-turn conversation history (bounded 3-5 exchanges). (6) Add loading state to the page (currently synchronous, no spinner). dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK Alloggiati JSONP — government submission promise hangs indefinitely, no timeout

dispatches.area_anchor: Reception offline sync — write queue bypass, no idempotency, no cache, no auth fallback
evidence_refs: operator-stated: useActivitiesMutations.ts, useChangeBookingDatesMutator.ts, useBookingMutations.ts write directly to Firebase — no queue-first path through existing receptionDb.ts + syncManager.ts + useOfflineSync.ts primitives
operator-stated: no idempotency keys or deterministic client IDs for create operations — retries duplicate writes
operator-stated: multi-path update(ref(database), updates) pattern not representable in current IndexedDB queue — atomic cross-path writes cannot be deferred safely
operator-stated: no conflict policy per domain — last-write-wins unsafe for financial amounts
operator-stated: no read-through cache for critical views — staff cannot work from last-known data when offline
operator-stated: no sync status UI showing pending/failed/retry write queue state or operator recovery actions
operator-stated: firebaseAuth.ts + AuthContext.tsx — profile fetch failure returns null, drops staff to unauthenticated mid-session
repo-observation: app shell offline support exists (sw.js, offline.html, ServiceWorkerRegistration.tsx, OfflineIndicator.tsx) but mutation paths do not route through IndexedDB queue
dispatches.current_truth: The reception app has offline infrastructure stubs: a service worker + offline page + banner exists, and IndexedDB queue/caching primitives (receptionDb.ts, syncManager.ts, useOfflineSync.ts) exist. However, all mutation paths (activities, bookings, date changes, financial transactions, loans) write directly to Firebase/Realtime DB without routing through the queue. Offline writes are silently lost. No idempotency key scheme exists for create operations. Multi-path Firebase atomics (update(ref(database), updates)) cannot be queued in the current schema. Auth can drop staff to unauthenticated when a profile fetch fails during network interruption. No read-through cache exists for critical views and no sync queue status is surfaced in the UI.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find: (1) audit receptionDb.ts, syncManager.ts, useOfflineSync.ts to understand current queue schema, sync trigger mechanisms, and gaps; (2) identify all mutation hooks that bypass the queue with call-site inventory; (3) design offline-aware mutation gateway interface — queue-first, sync-on-resume; (4) determine idempotency key scheme for create operations (deterministic client IDs); (5) assess how multi-path Firebase update() atomics can be serialized in IndexedDB without losing atomicity guarantees; (6) define read-through cache strategy for critical views (bookings, guests, rooms); (7) define per-domain conflict policy options (last-write-wins acceptable for activity flags, not for financial amounts); (8) scope sync status UI requirements (pending count, failed items, retry controls, recovery actions); (9) scope auth/profile fallback (cache profile on login, serve from cache when fetch fails, maintain session); (10) produce a phased, sequenced plan.

Operator policy: English is the default language. Italian already in place is acceptable but must not be the only option.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK reception stock count — no variance reason codes
dispatches.current_truth: BatchStockCount records a quantity delta when a count is submitted but captures no reason code for the variance. Operators cannot distinguish spoilage, staff comp, breakage, or theft from each other in the ledger. The existing ledger schema already has a 'reason' field used by waste entries; count entries leave it blank.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Add a reason-code prompt (dropdown or short free-text) to the BatchStockCount variance confirmation step. Only trigger the prompt when the count delta is negative. Persist the reason on the ledger entry. Surface the reason in StockManagement variance breakdown view.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: worldclass audit gap — no reason codes on count variance

completed_by.outcome: Reception stock count variance reason codes added.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK reception EOD — float set missing from close-out sequence
dispatches.current_truth: FloatEntryModal exists as a standalone component but is not wired into the EOD close-out sequence. When a shift closes, there is no prompted step to set the opening float for the next shift. Staff must remember to do this separately. EodChecklistContent does not include float status.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Integrate a 'Set opening float' step into the EOD close-out sequence (after till close, before the checklist marks complete). The float amount should default to the standard float value, be editable, and be persisted to Firebase. EodChecklistContent should reflect float-set status.

completed_by.outcome: EOD float-set step added to close-out sequence: float amount captured and stored in EOD record.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK reception EOD — no one-tap clean close confirmation
dispatches.current_truth: EodChecklistContent is a read-only status view showing till/safe/stock complete-or-incomplete. When all three sections are complete and balanced, there is no confirmation action — no 'Day closed' state, no stored record, no one-tap confirmation. Managers must navigate away and rely on memory that the day is done.
dispatches.next_scope_now: When all three EOD checklist items are complete, surface a 'Confirm day closed' button. On confirmation, write a day-closed record to Firebase with timestamp and confirming user. EodChecklistContent should show a 'Day closed' state with timestamp if a record exists for today. This is the 'offline-readable stored summary' requirement from the worldclass goal.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: worldclass audit gap — no stored day-closed confirmation

dispatches.current_truth: xa-uploader submission route is synchronous (timeout risk for large batches), has no concurrency lock (duplicate submissions possible), client draft schema diverges from API Zod schema, catalog KV key lacks TTL so stale data persists indefinitely, and submission errors are not logged or surfaced.

All implementable on Cloudflare free tier.

dispatches.area_anchor: lp-do-ideas trial pipeline — fact-find execution not guaranteed after routing assignment
dispatches.current_truth: When a dispatch is routed to lp-do-fact-find and processed_by is set in queue-state.json, the actual lp-do-fact-find invocation is not guaranteed — 19 of 43 selected dispatches have processed_by set but no fact-find.md on disk. The queue records routing intent but does not enforce execution.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Ensure that when a dispatch transitions to processed_by, the lp-do-fact-find invocation is atomic with the queue state write — if execution fails, the queue state must not be written. Alternatively, add an execution-verification step that detects processed_by-without-artifact dispatches and re-queues them. This is a reliability fix, not an autonomy change.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: funnel diagnosis — 19/43 selected dispatches have processed_by set but no fact-find.md on disk; execution not guaranteed after routing assignment

completed_by.outcome: lp-do-ideas fact-find execution guarantee built: queue-check gate and auto-execution protocol shipped.

dispatches.area_anchor: lp-do-ideas trial pipeline — build completion not auto-synced to queue state
dispatches.current_truth: When a plan completes the full build + archive cycle, the originating dispatch in queue-state.json is not automatically marked completed. The completed_by field must be written manually. 5 dispatches from brikette-sales-funnel-analysis are fully built and archived but still show queue_state: auto_executed. This means completion rate metrics undercount real throughput and the queue accumulates stale entries.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Add a queue-state completion hook to the lp-do-build post-build flow: after archiving a plan, look up any dispatches whose processed_by.fact_find_slug matches the feature slug and write completed_by { plan_path, completed_at, outcome }. This should be deterministic and idempotent — safe to re-run if the plan slug is already completed.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: funnel diagnosis — 5 dispatches from brikette-sales-funnel-analysis are fully built and archived but queue_state still shows auto_executed, not completed

completed_by.outcome: Added markDispatchesCompleted() module and step 7.5 to lp-do-build post-build flow. Queue-state completion is now automated.

dispatches.area_anchor: XA catalog workflow — live product edit can unpublish

A single incomplete save can silently remove a product from the storefront.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find to design a safety model for live product editing — options include last-published snapshot preservation, edit-in-draft-copy pattern, or save-time warnings when edits would demote a live product.

dispatches.why: A single accidental incomplete save can silently remove a product from the live storefront. This is a data-loss risk that grows as the catalog scales — one wrong edit during a price update session could take multiple products offline without warning.

intended_outcome.statement: Editing a live product cannot silently unpublish it — the system either preserves the live version, warns before demotion, or requires explicit confirmation to unpublish.

Recurring or high-severity findings that warrant dedicated investigation are lost in the aggregate report.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find the bridge from bug-scan findings to ideas dispatch: severity threshold for automatic dispatch, grouping strategy (per-finding vs per-file vs per-rule), deduplication against existing dispatches, and integration point (build completion step vs separate CLI).
adjacent_later: Extend to CI test failure signals as a second code-quality dispatch source.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: codebase is a standing data source the loop should learn from — bug scan is the nearest bridge point
agent-verified: bug-scan.ts produces BugScanIdeaArtifact JSON with structured findings (ruleId, severity, file, suggestion)
agent-verified: generate-process-improvements.ts ingests bug-scan findings but only outputs to process-improvements.user.html
agent-verified: ideas pipeline has zero code-artifact consumption paths
dispatches.why: The bug scanner already identifies code-level risks at every build but the findings only appear in an aggregate HTML report. High-severity or recurring findings that deserve their own investigation and plan never enter the ideas pipeline — they are visible but not actionable through the standard workflow.

intended_outcome.statement: Bug scan findings above a severity threshold automatically produce dispatch packets in the ideas queue, so recurring code-quality issues get dedicated investigation and resolution through the standard planning workflow.

completed_by.outcome: Codebase-signals bridge wired into process-improvements; bug-scan and structural-diff signals now emit dispatch candidates

dispatches.area_anchor: Codebase signals — structural changes not detected by ideas pipeline
dispatches.current_truth: The ideas pipeline is designed exclusively for markdown-based standing artifacts. Code structural changes — new routes, API endpoint additions, schema modifications, dependency updates, component additions — produce zero signal in the ideas pipeline. The orchestrator expects pre-computed markdown sections and fail-closes on unregistered artifacts. No git diff parsing, no CI signal routing, no code-structure change detection exists. The codebase is the richest and most frequently-changing standing data source but is entirely invisible to the loop.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find the codebase-as-standing-data model: which code structural changes are meaningful signals (route additions needing measurement, API changes needing documentation, dependency updates needing compatibility checks), what detection mechanism fits (git diff analysis, AST-level parsing, CI output scraping), and what the minimum viable code-signal-to-dispatch bridge looks like.
adjacent_later: Evaluate whether code signals should use the existing standing registry model or a separate lightweight event bus to avoid registry bloat.
Consider whether test coverage changes (new uncovered code) should be a signal category.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: the codebase itself is standing data the loop should learn from
agent-verified: standing registry has zero code artifact entries — all 15 are markdown docs
agent-verified: ideas orchestrator expects pre-computed markdown changed_sections, fail-closes on unknown artifacts
agent-verified: no git diff parsing, no CI failure routing, no code-structure change detection in pipeline
agent-verified: deliverable family 'code-change' exists in dispatch schema but nothing produces it
dispatches.why: The codebase changes with every build and those changes contain the richest signal about what's happening in each business — new functionality that needs measurement, API changes that need documentation, dependency updates that need review. Currently the loop only sees document-level changes and is completely blind to the code-level reality of each business.

intended_outcome.statement: A defined model for codebase-as-standing-data with at least one working signal bridge (code structural changes producing dispatch candidates), so the ideas pipeline can detect and route follow-on work from code evolution.

completed_by.outcome: Codebase-signals bridge wired into process-improvements; bug-scan and structural-diff signals now emit dispatch candidates

dispatches.area_anchor: Agent sessions — walkthrough and testing findings evaporate after session
dispatches.current_truth: Signal capture only covers planned work: build-output bridge and ideas-trial bridge both emit MetaObservation records, but only from lp-do-build completions and dispatch events. Agent walkthroughs, manual testing sessions, operator-directed investigations, and 'list any issues' explorations produce concrete findings (bugs, broken flows, UX gaps, missing functionality) that exist only in the conversation window and vanish when the session ends. The tools-meta-reflect skill updates docs but never emits observations or dispatches. The MEMORY.md system captures some learnings but has no bridge to the ideas pipeline. Conversation transcripts in .claude/projects/ are never parsed for signals. The MetaObservation schema has a session_id field but it's auto-generated, not linked to actual Claude Code sessions.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Fact-find the agent-session-to-signal bridge: how to capture findings from unplanned explorations (walkthroughs, testing, audits) and route them into the ideas pipeline. Key design questions: automatic extraction from conversation transcripts vs explicit emission at session end, observation granularity (per-finding vs per-session summary), deduplication against existing dispatches, and whether this integrates via the existing MetaObservation schema or needs a new signal type.
adjacent_later: Evaluate periodic transcript mining for recurring patterns across sessions (e.g., same bug found multiple times, same workaround applied repeatedly).
Consider whether agent-discovered issues should route directly to dispatch queue or through observations.jsonl first for pattern aggregation.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: agent walkthroughs like 'step through the uploader app, simulate uploading, track to xa-b, list issues' are a primary issue discovery method — findings are never captured
agent-verified: only build-output bridge and ideas-trial bridge emit MetaObservation records — both require planned work
agent-verified: tools-meta-reflect updates docs only, never emits observations or dispatches
agent-verified: MEMORY.md has no bridge to ideas pipeline or observations.jsonl
agent-verified: .claude/projects/ conversation transcripts are never parsed by any startup-loop tool
agent-verified: MetaObservation schema has session_id field but it's auto-generated, not linked to Claude Code sessions
dispatches.why: Agent conversations are the most frequent source of issue discovery — operators routinely ask agents to walk through apps, test flows, and list problems. These findings are concrete and actionable but evaporate when the session ends. The loop's self-improvement mechanism is blind to its richest signal source because signal capture only covers structured, plan-based work. Every unplanned walkthrough that finds a bug is a missed dispatch.

intended_outcome.statement: Agent session findings (bugs, broken flows, gaps discovered during walkthroughs and testing) are captured and routed into the ideas pipeline, so the loop can learn from its most frequent discovery method rather than losing those signals at session end.

completed_by.outcome: Agent-session bridge extracts findings from transcripts and emits dispatch candidates; wired into process-improvements

dispatches.area_anchor: XA uploader — no auto-save after image upload
dispatches.current_truth: After R2 image upload succeeds, the returned key is appended to React state via onChange() but the product is NOT auto-saved to the server. If the vendor navigates away, the R2 key is lost and the image becomes an orphaned R2 object. The draft→ready transition (derivePublishState) also does not fire until the next manual save.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Add auto-save after successful image upload so the R2 key is persisted and derivePublishState() transitions draft→ready immediately.
adjacent_later: Image deletion from R2 when product is deleted
Unsaved-changes warning on navigation
evidence_refs: operator-stated: Post-build simulation found image upload appends R2 key to React state but does not persist to server — key lost on navigation, draft→ready transition delayed
dispatches.why: Image upload completes successfully but the R2 key is only held in React state — not persisted. Vendor loses upload on navigation and the draft→ready publish transition doesn't fire until a separate manual save. This breaks the intended upload-makes-product-publishable workflow.

intended_outcome.statement: After a successful image upload, the product draft is auto-saved so the R2 key is persisted and the draft→ready transition fires immediately without requiring a separate manual save.

dispatches.area_anchor: XA catalog sync — imageRoles dropped during publish
dispatches.current_truth: buildMediaEntries() in catalogDraftToContract.ts takes imageFiles and imageAltTexts but NOT imageRoles. The CatalogMediaEntry type is { type, path, altText } with no role field. Roles are stored in the draft but dropped when the catalog is published to JSON. xa-b has no semantic image ordering — it renders images in array order.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Investigate adding role field to CatalogMediaEntry and propagating imageRoles through buildMediaEntries() to the published catalog.
adjacent_later: xa-b semantic image ordering by role (hero vs detail vs lifestyle)
evidence_refs: operator-stated: Pre-existing gap — imageRoles stored in draft but dropped by buildMediaEntries during sync. xa-b has no way to distinguish front from detail images
dispatches.why: Image roles (front/side/detail/lifestyle) are captured during upload but discarded when the catalog is published. xa-b cannot semantically order images (e.g. hero shot first) because role metadata is lost in the sync pipeline. Pre-existing gap surfaced during R2 image upload post-build analysis.

intended_outcome.statement: imageRoles propagated through buildMediaEntries and published catalog JSON so xa-b can order images semantically by role.

dispatches.area_anchor: XA R2 storage — orphaned objects on re-upload
dispatches.current_truth: R2 key format uses timestamp prefix ({storefront}/{slug}/{timestamp}-{role}.{ext}). Re-uploading the same role for a product creates a new R2 object — the old one is orphaned (still stored, still costs 10GB free tier quota, but never referenced). No cleanup mechanism exists.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Investigate adding R2 object deletion when a new image replaces an existing role for the same product, plus periodic orphan scanning.
adjacent_later: R2 storage usage monitoring/alerting approaching 10GB free tier limit
evidence_refs: operator-stated: Timestamp-prefixed R2 keys mean re-uploads create new objects and orphan old ones. No deletion mechanism. 10GB free tier is the binding storage constraint.
dispatches.why: R2 free tier is 10GB. Each image re-upload orphans the previous object. At scale this wastes storage toward the hard free-tier cap with no way to reclaim it. Low urgency at current catalog size but will compound.

intended_outcome.statement: When a new image replaces an existing role for a product, the previous R2 object is deleted. Orphaned storage is bounded.

dispatches.area_anchor: XA uploader — no image preview + generic alt text
dispatches.current_truth: After uploading an image, the vendor sees 'Upload successful' text but no thumbnail preview of what was uploaded. Alt text is auto-generated as generic '{role} view' (e.g. 'front view') instead of including the product name. Both gaps are in CatalogProductImagesFields.client.tsx.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Add thumbnail grid showing uploaded images with roles, and generate product-specific alt text using draft.title.
adjacent_later: Image reordering drag-and-drop UI
Image deletion from upload panel
evidence_refs: operator-stated: No visual confirmation of upload — only text status. Alt text is generic 'front view' instead of '{product name} — front view', which is poor for SEO.
dispatches.why: Vendors have no visual feedback after uploading product images — only a text status message. Combined with generic alt text that omits the product name, this creates both a UX gap (can't verify upload) and an SEO gap (poor image alt text in published catalog).

intended_outcome.statement: Uploaded images shown as thumbnails with role labels in the upload panel. Auto-generated alt text includes product title for SEO.

dispatches.next_scope_now: Create R2 bucket, enable public access, update xa-b wrangler.toml with actual public URL, deploy both apps.

dispatches.why: The R2 image upload feature is code-complete but cannot be used until the R2 bucket is created, public access enabled, and the actual public URL configured. intended_outcome.statement: R2 bucket created, public access enabled, actual public URL set in xa-b wrangler.toml. Both xa-uploader and xa-b deployed with working image upload and display.

dispatches.area_anchor: BOS build-completion — deterministic template pre-fill
dispatches.current_truth: The lp-do-build plan completion sequence (steps 1-8) burns 7-15k tokens per build cycle. 60-65% of results-review content is deterministic template fill (None categories, standing updates, pattern-reflection recurrence counts, intended-outcome verdicts for clean builds). These sections are generated by LLM but could be pre-filled by TS scripts that check build diffs, registry paths, and task completion status.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Build a deterministic results-review pre-fill script that: (1) auto-checks 5 New Idea Candidate categories against build diff and pre-fills None, (2) auto-detects standing updates via git-diff/registry intersection, (3) auto-counts pattern-reflection recurrence via archive grep, (4) auto-fills intended-outcome verdict when all tasks are Complete with no scope deviations. LLM only writes non-None idea descriptions and notes for non-clean verdicts.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: 60-65% of results-review is deterministic template fill — None categories, standing updates, pattern-reflection counts
operator-stated: 5 scan categories (data source, package, skill, loop process, AI-to-mechanistic) can be auto-checked against build diff
operator-stated: standing updates are a file-path intersection between git diff and standing-registry.json
operator-stated: pattern-reflection recurrence is a grep count across _archive/*/results-review.user.md
operator-stated: intended-outcome verdict is Met when all tasks Complete + no scope deviations
dispatches.why: Each build cycle burns 7-15k tokens on results-review, pattern-reflection, and standing-updates sections that are 60-65% boilerplate. The LLM generates None entries, counts recurrences, and fills verdicts that a TS script could compute deterministically from build diffs and task status.

intended_outcome.statement: A deterministic pre-fill script generates 60%+ of results-review, pattern-reflection, and standing-updates content from build artifacts, reducing per-build token consumption by ~55%.

completed_by.outcome: Deterministic pre-fill scripts generate 60%+ of results-review and pattern-reflection content from build artifacts, reducing per-build token consumption by ~55%.

dispatches.area_anchor: BOS ideas intake — structured evidence questions

The SKILL.md documents 10 evidence fields but relies on LLM judgment to detect when to ask for them.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Replace free-text signal detection with 5 targeted yes/no questions asked in sequence. Only ask follow-up for yes answers. This halves intake token cost while capturing the same evidence fields more reliably.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: intake burns 2-4k tokens per operator idea on free-text signal detection
operator-stated: 10 evidence fields documented in SKILL.md but detection is LLM-judgment-based
operator-stated: 5 structured yes/no questions could replace free-text detection
dispatches.why: Operator intake spends tokens on LLM-based signal detection that could be 5 structured questions. The evidence fields are well-defined — the gap is that the skill relies on LLM judgment to decide when to ask, rather than always asking a short structured sequence.

intended_outcome.statement: lp-do-ideas operator intake uses structured yes/no questions for evidence field capture, reducing per-intake token cost by ~50% while improving evidence capture reliability.

dispatches.next_scope_now: Add New In badge to product cards for recently added items and consider quick-view for non-clothing categories.

dispatches.why: The site prominently features a New In section but product cards have no visual indicator to distinguish new arrivals when they appear in mixed listings. This reduces the effectiveness of the new-arrivals merchandising strategy.

intended_outcome.statement: Product cards display a New In badge for recently added items, using the xa-b achromatic design language with proper visual hierarchy.

evidence_refs: operator-stated: empty states across cart, filters, and wishlist are plain text with no visual treatment

intended_outcome.statement: Empty states across cart, filter no-results, and wishlist display styled messaging with clear CTAs, consistent with xa-b luxury-editorial design language.

dispatches.area_anchor: BOS ideas-trial — static keyword list, no calibration feedback
evidence_refs: operator-stated: T1_SEMANTIC_KEYWORDS is a static 42+ term list with no calibration from queue-state dispatch outcomes (confirmed vs skipped)
operator-stated: draft_ranker_calibrate serves the email pipeline, not the ideas pipeline
operator-stated: candidate for deterministic script with feedback loop from queue-state outcomes
dispatches.why: Source-trigger classification uses a static keyword list (T1_SEMANTIC_KEYWORDS, 42+ terms) that doesn't grow when new business domains are added. Confirmed vs skipped dispatch outcomes in queue-state are not fed back to adjust keyword weights, so routing accuracy degrades over time.

intended_outcome.statement: Produce a deterministic calibration script that analyzes confirmed vs skipped dispatches in queue-state.json and adjusts T1 semantic keyword weights accordingly, creating a feedback loop from dispatch outcomes to routing accuracy.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK private-accom — broken meta tags + noindex regression
dispatches.current_truth: Page meta title/description render raw i18n keys (apartment.meta.title). Page has noindex,follow blocking all search indexing. getTranslations with optional:true silently fails.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Investigate i18n resolution failure in generateMetadata and determine whether noindex should be removed for this landing page.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: staging audit of 8a8586f7.brikette-website.pages.dev/en/book-private-accomodations — title/description show raw i18n keys, noindex directive present
dispatches.why: Private accommodation booking page is invisible to search engines due to broken meta tags (raw i18n keys) and noindex directive. This page is intended as a search landing page and funnel bridge for direct private room bookings — the highest-value conversion path.

intended_outcome.statement: Page serves correct meta title/description and is indexable by search engines, enabling organic discovery of private accommodation booking.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK private-accom — SSR content gap, thin landing copy
dispatches.current_truth: Zero H1/H2/H3 in server-rendered HTML. Main content (rooms, FAQ, images) is client-rendered only — invisible to crawlers. No intro paragraph, feature highlights, or trust signals for landing page role.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Add server-rendered headings, descriptive intro copy with apartment features (85sqm, kitchen, 2 bathrooms, sea view), trust signals, and ensure key content is in initial HTML.
adjacent_later: Internal cross-links to dorms comparison, experiences, location guide
evidence_refs: operator-stated: staging audit shows zero headings in server HTML, no content images SSR, no descriptive copy for landing page
dispatches.why: Without server-rendered content, the page cannot rank or engage users arriving from search. The apartment is the highest-margin product — its landing page needs descriptive copy and proper heading hierarchy to convert organic traffic.

intended_outcome.statement: Page has H1 heading, intro paragraph with feature highlights, and room content visible in server-rendered HTML — confirmed by crawl test showing structured headings.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK private-accom — structured data + URL + SEO signals
dispatches.current_truth: No JSON-LD structured data (LodgingBusiness/Product). URL misspelled as accomodations (one m). OG image is generic facade.avif. No hreflang x-default. No internal cross-links to related pages.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Add JSON-LD structured data, fix URL spelling with redirect chain, set apartment-specific OG image, add hreflang x-default, and add in-content cross-links.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: staging audit shows no structured data, URL typo, generic OG image, missing hreflang x-default, no internal links
dispatches.why: Rich snippets (price, availability, rating) and correct URL spelling improve click-through from search results. The URL typo hurts keyword matching on the canonical URL for accommodation searches.

intended_outcome.statement: Page has JSON-LD LodgingBusiness schema, canonical URL corrected to accommodations (double m) with 301 redirect, apartment-specific OG image, and hreflang x-default tag.

dispatches.area_anchor: startup-loop module — cmd-advance monolith split

evidence_refs: operator-stated: A single 532-line module in the startup-loop command path concentrates complexity and raises edit risk.
dispatches.why: A single 532-line module in the startup-loop command path concentrates complexity and raises edit risk.

intended_outcome.statement: Split cmd-advance.md into focused modules with unchanged external behavior and clearer maintenance boundaries.

dispatches.area_anchor: skill logic layer — deterministic extraction program
dispatches.current_truth: Current audit heuristics (H0-H3) are structural only. No scoring for deterministic logic extraction into TS/JSON/YAML, and no anti-gaming check for line-count compliance without simplification.

evidence_refs: operator-stated: Without explicit extraction goals, line reductions can be satisfied by moving prose around rather than moving deterministic rules into executable/data artifacts.
dispatches.why: Without explicit extraction goals, line reductions can be satisfied by moving prose around rather than moving deterministic rules into executable/data artifacts.

intended_outcome.statement: Define and execute extraction of deterministic rules, decision tables, and contracts into typed .ts or data (.json/.yaml) artifacts with validation tests.

dispatches.area_anchor: Reception booking email flow — false success and activity drift
dispatches.current_truth: Booking email failures are swallowed while UI reports success and logs activity 26.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Harden hook return contract and caller handling so success is only reported after confirmed draft creation.
dispatches.why: Operators currently get false positive success signals for a guest-facing communication workflow.

intended_outcome.statement: Booking email draft success state is truthful and activity logging only occurs on confirmed success.

dispatches.area_anchor: Reception cancellation flow — activity outcomes not verified
dispatches.current_truth: Cancellation currently accepts partial activity/email failures without explicit surfaced outcome.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Require occupant-level activity result verification and explicit failure reporting.
dispatches.why: Booking cancellation status should not silently hide failed downstream activity/email operations.

intended_outcome.statement: Cancellation flow surfaces partial failures deterministically and cannot silently pass.

dispatches.area_anchor: Prime extension resolution — status can advance after failed write
dispatches.current_truth: Prime request resolver can mark extension approved after booking-date mutation fails internally.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Make booking-date mutation fail-closed and gate status transition on mutation success.
dispatches.why: Prime request status must remain consistent with actual booking state.

intended_outcome.statement: Prime extension status only advances when booking-date mutation succeeds.

dispatches.area_anchor: Booking date mutation — sequential writes create partial state risk
dispatches.current_truth: Booking date mutation performs dependent writes in sequence across multiple paths.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Consolidate dependent non-financial node writes into atomic multi-path update.
dispatches.why: Partial writes can leave booking/checkin/checkout/activity nodes inconsistent.

intended_outcome.statement: Booking date changes are written atomically across core dependent nodes.

dispatches.area_anchor: Financial room mutation — read-merge-write race under concurrency
dispatches.current_truth: Financial room updater uses get+set merge path vulnerable to concurrent overwrite.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Adopt transaction-based write semantics for financial room mutation path.
dispatches.why: Financial integrity requires concurrency-safe updates.

intended_outcome.statement: Concurrent financial writes no longer lose transactions via stale overwrite.

dispatches.area_anchor: Booking email API payload — semantic validation insufficient
dispatches.current_truth: Booking email payload currently allows non-URL occupantLinks.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Harden route + MCP tool schema to enforce URL semantics for occupant links.
dispatches.why: Weak semantic validation permits malformed outbound guest link payloads.

intended_outcome.statement: Booking email payload contract enforces URL-valid occupant links end-to-end.

dispatches.area_anchor: Reception integrity paths — verification coverage gaps
dispatches.current_truth: Critical integrity paths lack targeted regression assertions for silent-failure behavior.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Add focused tests across modified reception hooks/routes for issues 2-7.
dispatches.why: Without regression coverage these high-risk integrity bugs can return silently.

intended_outcome.statement: Targeted regression suite covers all hardened risk paths from issues 2-7.

dispatches.area_anchor: Booking modal save flow — closes before mutation confirmation
dispatches.current_truth: Booking modal closes immediately after firing updateBookingDates without awaiting outcome.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Await mutation in modal and keep dialog open on errors with explicit operator feedback.
evidence_refs: re-audit finding: booking modal fail-closed gap
dispatches.why: UI currently allows false-success perception when save fails asynchronously.

intended_outcome.statement: Booking modal save is fail-closed and only closes after confirmed mutation success.

dispatches.area_anchor: Booking modal occupant validation — sentinel fallback write risk
dispatches.current_truth: Missing occupant id falls back to unknown_occupant and can write to invalid data paths.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Block save when occupant id is absent and surface validation error to operator.
evidence_refs: re-audit finding: occupant id validation gap
dispatches.why: Invalid fallback writes create silent data pollution.

intended_outcome.statement: Booking save path enforces valid occupant id and refuses invalid writes.

dispatches.area_anchor: Extension mutation consistency — date/financial split operation risk
dispatches.current_truth: Extension booking-date update and financial recording remain split operations with divergence risk.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Add compensation control so financial writes are neutralized if downstream booking mutation fails.
evidence_refs: re-audit finding: high-stakes financial consistency risk
dispatches.why: Financial consistency is high-stakes and requires explicit recovery behavior.

intended_outcome.statement: Extension flow has compensating control to avoid unresolved financial/date divergence.

dispatches.area_anchor: Extension modal multi-occupant flow — partial commit ambiguity
dispatches.current_truth: Multi-occupant extension can partially apply before failure without explicit partial-outcome messaging.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Serialize updates and provide explicit partial-failure reporting while suppressing downstream side effects.
evidence_refs: re-audit finding: extension partial-failure handling gap
dispatches.why: Operators need deterministic recovery signals when multi-occupant updates fail.

intended_outcome.statement: Multi-occupant extension flow surfaces partial outcomes explicitly and avoids silent drift.

dispatches.area_anchor: Booking email activity logging — non-authoritative scope source
dispatches.current_truth: Activity logging currently uses local email-map scope rather than authoritative send result scope.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Return occupant ids from send hook and log activity using that authoritative list.
dispatches.why: Audit trail should reflect confirmed draft occupant scope.

intended_outcome.statement: Email draft activity logging uses authoritative occupant set returned by booking-email hook.

dispatches.area_anchor: Cancellation contract — partial failure can be ignored by callers
dispatches.current_truth: Cancellation partial failures are returned as data and can be accidentally ignored by future callers.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Promote partial-failure path to thrown error contract with details for fail-closed handling.
dispatches.why: Critical cancellation side effects should not silently pass due unchecked return value.

intended_outcome.statement: Cancellation partial failure becomes fail-closed error contract.

dispatches.area_anchor: Reception re-audit verification — failure-path coverage gaps
dispatches.current_truth: Failure-path tests still miss booking modal reject flow, extension partial failure messaging, and email button scope behavior.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Add targeted test cases for each remaining failure-path contract.
dispatches.why: Without coverage, silent-failure regressions can reappear.

intended_outcome.statement: Failure-path regression coverage is closed for re-audit follow-up hardening.

dispatches.current_truth: Reception flows still allowed duplicate activity logging, non-throwing mutation failures, split-write drift, and recipient-fetch failure misclassification.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Harden prepayment/status/checkout/email contracts to fail closed and add behavior-level verification.

Harden YOY API and auth cache fallback residual integrity gaps.

dispatches.why: Silent failures in operator-critical reception workflows can desynchronize UI decisions from persisted state and hide delivery failures.

intended_outcome.statement: Reception mutation paths fail closed with compensation where needed and behavior tests cover the hardened contracts.

dispatches.area_anchor: Reception re-audit opportunities — fail-closed activity and async integrity gaps
dispatches.current_truth: Reception re-audit still shows fail-open activity wrappers, unchecked mutation success results, and async fire-and-forget writes that can silently lose audit/financial side effects.
dispatches.next_scope_now: Harden activity mutation result handling to fail closed, remove fire-and-forget critical writes, and close coverage gaps for non-throwing failure paths.
adjacent_later: Consolidate multi-write financial mutations under shared fail-closed transactional helpers.
Add component-level regression tests for keycard and loans mutation orchestration.

dispatches.why: Critical front-desk flows can report success while activity or financial side effects fail silently, which undermines operator trust and data integrity.

intended_outcome.statement: Reception mutation flows fail closed on non-throwing mutation failures and async side effects are either confirmed or surfaced as explicit errors with regression coverage.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK email pipeline — intent extraction collapses clause-level questions, double-counts intents
evidence_refs: operator-stated: simulation audit — four representative email threads revealed punctuation-based question extraction merges clause-level intents into one, and request extractors double-count question stems
dispatches.why: Mixed-intent guest emails (e.g. availability + cancellation policy in one message) currently collapse to a single dominant answer path, leaving one sub-question unaddressed. The extraction layer must atomize compound clauses before scenario ranking to prevent routine pre-booking emails from dropping a question.

intended_outcome.statement: Clause-level intent atomization and span-based deduplication are implemented in draft-interpret so that compound questions produce one intent object per clause, and interrogative requests are deduplicated by source span rather than counted twice.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK email pipeline — template ranker accepts weak matches, booking_monitor triggers too broadly
evidence_refs: operator-stated: simulation audit — unknown-facility question (rooftop pool) received a fluent but unrelated policy template; any mention of 'booking' enables booking_monitor path causing false reference-link failures on plain FAQ drafts
dispatches.why: Template-ranker always selects candidate 0 from BM25 results even when the match is below any useful similarity threshold, producing confident-sounding but wrong answers for unknown topics. Separately, booking_monitor activates on any mention of 'booking' rather than only on operational booking actions, causing quality checks to demand reference links on ordinary FAQ drafts and generating false failures.

intended_outcome.statement: Per-question confidence floor and category-compatibility checks gate template block acceptance in template-ranker and draft-generate; below-threshold questions emit follow_up_required with no template text. booking_monitor is scoped to operational booking actions (not informational mentions), removing false reference-link quality failures from FAQ drafts.

dispatches.area_anchor: BRIK email pipeline — quality gate advisory-only; coverage scoring lexical-only
evidence_refs: operator-stated: simulation audit — draft_generate returns a full draft even when quality.passed is false; the hard stop exists only in the ops-inbox workflow doc, not in the tool boundary; coverage scoring is keyword-overlap only and marks valid answers partial
dispatches.why: The quality gate being advisory at the tool boundary means a failed draft can still be submitted as a Gmail draft if the operator misses the quality.passed flag — the safety guarantee only exists in a workflow doc, not enforced in code. Separately, the lexical-only coverage scorer generates false negatives on valid answers that paraphrase rather than echo question keywords, creating unnecessary operator patch churn.

intended_outcome.statement: draft_generate exposes a machine-enforced delivery_status field (ready | needs_patch | blocked) and refuses Gmail draft creation when blocked. Coverage scoring incorporates category-aware synonym packs and topic-label checks so that paraphrased correct answers are not marked partial.
